item.title: Phage intervention improves colitis and response to corticosteroids by attenuating virulence of Crohn's disease-associated bacteria.
item.description: Science translational medicine - Adherent-invasive Escherichia coli (AIEC) exhibits proinflammatory properties and has been implicated in the pathogenesis of Crohn's disease (CD), a form of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Antibi...

item.title: Early real-world effectiveness of mirikizumab in a difficult-to-treat ulcerative colitis cohort.
Effectiveness and safety of Ustekinumab in older patients with Crohn´s disease with high comorbidity burden: a real-world study.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Elderly patients are an increasingly relevant group in inflammatory bowel disease, yet clinical trials often exclude them. Ustekinumab might be a suitable treatment due to its adequate clinical and...

item.title: Cancer risk with methotrexate-TNF antagonist combination therapy compared to TNF-antagonist monotherapy in IBD.

item.title: Assessing trial eligibility and real-world response to vedolizumab and ustekinumab in patients with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - Biologic therapies for patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) were approved via large randomized controlled trials (RCTs) with strict criteria for patient enrollment. As newer IBD medicatio...

item.title: Long-term clinical and endoscopic efficacy of vedolizumab and therapeutic persistence in patients with ulcerative colitis in China: a multicenter observational study.
item.description: Intestinal research - Limited data are available on the long-term efficacy of vedolizumab (VDZ) in Asian patients with ulcerative colitis (UC), particularly regarding endoscopic outcomes. In this multicenter, retrospect...

item.title: Effectiveness and safety of mirikizumab in multirefractory ulcerative colitis: analysis from a real-world cohort.
item.description: Digestive and liver disease : official journal of the Italian Society of Gastroenterology and the Italian Association for the Study of the Liver - Mirikizumab is a selective anti-IL-23 monoclonal antibody approved for moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (UC). Evidence regarding its efficacy mostly derives from registration trials, as real-w...

item.title: Randomized trial comparing 5-year follow-up of first-line infliximab to conventional therapy in paediatric Crohn's disease.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - The TISKids study previously demonstrated that first-line infliximab (FL-IFX) was more effective than conventional treatment in achieving clinical remission without treatment escalation at 52 weeks...

item.title: Cytotoxic T-Lymphocyte-Associated Protein 4 Deficiency Colitis Masked by Recurrent Cytomegalovirus Colitis Successfully Treated With Vedolizumab.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated protein 4 (CTLA-4) deficiency is a primary immunodeficiency syndrome caused by mutations in the CTLA-4 gene. Its heterogeneous presentation, often with multisystem...

item.title: Higher body mass index is associated with lower drug concentrations in patients with inflammatory bowel disease treated with ustekinumab but not vedolizumab.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - There are limited data regarding the impact of body mass index (BMI) on pharmacokinetics in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) treated with vedolizumab (VDZ) or ustekinumab (UST). The a...

item.title: Comments on "Prevalence, patient characteristics and outcomes of high-dose upadacitinib in inflammatory bowel disease".

item.description: Frontiers in medicine - Review - Intestinal fibrosis remains one of the most challenging complications of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), frequently leading to bowel strictures, impaired intestinal function, and surgical interve...

item.title: Long-term drug persistence and clinical outcomes of ustekinumab in Crohn's disease: a multicenter real-world study from the Hokkaido phoenix cohort.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - Although ustekinumab (UST) is effective in Crohn's disease (CD), long-term real-world data beyond 5 years and its effects on extraintestinal manifestations remain limited. We evaluated UST's long-t...
